commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4470752dfcb64bc35749a4d4a6f4421722ebb69b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds persistent OpenAI Realtime disconnect handling, tears down the paired Twilio call, and adds socket-level and bridge regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level with high confidence: current main has no post-connect close propagation, and the added real-socket regression demonstrates the relevant WebSocket failure mechanics. This review did not independently reproduce the complete failure through a live OpenAI and Twilio call.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor must provide real behavior proof and remove the release-owned changelog line before a maintainer makes the final merge decision.

target.title: fix(openai): detect realtime socket loss and tear the call down instead of leaving dead air
target.body:
## Summary

**Classification: runtime bug fix — silent mid-call failure that strands live calls (dead air) and leaks per-call resources.**

After the OpenAI Realtime handshake, `connectOpenAIRealtime` never listens for `error` or `close` again (`src/openai.ts`): the only error listener is the handshake's `ws.once("error", reject)`, which after a successful open just dangles on a settled promise. Two consequences:

1. **Dead-air calls.** If OpenAI drops the WebSocket mid-call (network blip, server-side session teardown, idle timeout), the bridge never finds out. The Twilio leg stays up, the caller sits in silence indefinitely, nothing is written to `events.jsonl`, and the agent client (potentially a subprocess) and logger leak until Twilio eventually gives up. `ws` 8.x makes this fully silent: `send()` on a closed socket is a buffered no-op, so even continued caller audio produces no signal that the session is gone.
2. **A latent crash window.** The stale handshake listener swallows the *first* mid-call `error` event (reject on a settled promise) and is then removed — a subsequent `error` on that socket would be unlistened and take down the whole process, every concurrent call included.

### The fix

- `src/openai.ts`: on open, detach the handshake reject listener and attach a persistent `error` recorder (no socket error can ever be unlistened again) plus a `close` listener. The client gains `onClose(handler)` — close info is buffered if it arrives before registration, mirroring the existing buffered-server-events pattern. An optional `url` override allows pointing the client at alternate gateways and real local test servers.
- `src/server.ts`: the bridge registers `onClose` — it logs `openai.ws.closed` (with code/reason), runs the existing idempotent `cleanupCall()`, and closes the Twilio socket so the caller gets a clean call end instead of dead air. Self-initiated closes during normal teardown are ignored via the existing `closed` guard, so no double-cleanup.

Verified against the actual library behavior before scoping: `@fastify/websocket` 11.x already guards the Twilio socket's `error` event (index.js:130), so the Twilio leg needs no change — the fix stays confined to the OpenAI leg.

## Reproducibility — deterministic, real sockets

Two layers, same pattern as #8:

**`test/openai.test.ts`** exercises the **real client over real WebSocket connections** to a local `WebSocketServer` (no fakes on the transport under test): server-initiated close reaches the handler with code/reason; a close landing before registration is buffered, not lost; an abrupt TCP `terminate()` (no close frame) surfaces as `onClose` with 1006 instead of an unhandled `error`; `send()` after the drop is a no-op.

**`test/server-openai-disconnect.test.ts`** drives the real `handleTwilioSocket` through the production sequence (start → session.created → session.updated → connection close) and pins the contract: the disconnect is logged, the Twilio socket is closed exactly once, cleanup runs exactly once, late traffic is ignored, and an OpenAI close *after* Twilio-initiated teardown is a no-op (no re-close, no late log writes).

Against the unfixed code (`git checkout origin/main -- src/openai.ts src/server.ts`):

```
$ node --import tsx --test test/server-openai-disconnect.test.ts
not ok 1 - mid-call OpenAI disconnect tears the call down instead of leaving dead air
  failureType: 'testCodeFailure'
  error: 'the disconnect must be visible in the call log'
  name: 'AssertionError'
# tests 2
# pass 1
# fail 1
```

— the bridge observably does nothing when the session dies: no log entry, Twilio socket never closed, no cleanup. With the fix, all 4 + 2 new tests pass.

## Test plan — local run output (proof)

Exact CI pipeline (`lint` → `typecheck` → `test` → `build`) on both matrix versions.

### Node v22.22.3

```
$ pnpm run lint
All matched files use the correct format.
(oxlint --deny-warnings: clean exit)

$ pnpm run typecheck
> tsgo -p tsconfig.json --noEmit
(clean exit)

$ pnpm test
# tests 39
# pass 39
# fail 0

$ pnpm run build
ESM ⚡️ Build success in 42ms
```

### Node v24.16.0

```
$ pnpm test
ℹ tests 39
ℹ pass 39
ℹ fail 0
```

## Hosted workflow run — live proof (this exact head SHA, on GitHub-hosted runners)

The CI workflow also triggers on `push` (`branches: ["**"]`), so the exact head commit of this PR already ran the full pipeline end-to-end on GitHub's hosted infrastructure:

**Run:** https://github.com/joeVenner/vox/actions/runs/27427486978 (commit `4470752dfcb64bc35749a4d4a6f4421722ebb69b`, the current PR head) — ✅ both matrix jobs succeeded: `test (22)` and `test (24)`.

Live output from the hosted run confirming the new tests executed there:

```
test (22)  Run pnpm test       # tests 39
test (22)  Run pnpm test       # pass 39
test (22)  Run pnpm test       # fail 0
test (22)  Run pnpm run build  ESM ⚡️ Build success in 27ms
test (24)  Run pnpm test       ℹ tests 39
test (24)  Run pnpm test       ℹ pass 39
test (24)  Run pnpm test       ℹ fail 0
test (24)  Run pnpm run build  ESM ⚡️ Build success in 27ms
```

## Risk notes

- `onClose` is added as a **required** member of `OpenAIRealtimeClient`, so the type system forces every consumer (and test fake) to acknowledge the disconnect path; the two existing fakes gained a one-line stub.
- `vox simulate` constructs the same client but registers no `onClose`; its behavior is unchanged (a disconnect there already surfaces to the operator when the next send produces no response — wiring simulate teardown is a separate concern, happy to follow up).
- The new `url` option defaults to the existing hardcoded endpoint; no behavior change unless explicitly passed.
- Security: no dependency, workflow, permission, or secret-handling changes.
target.author: joevenner

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
